title: Neomammillaria sanluisensis
taxonomy: Neomammillaria sanluisensis is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the genus Neomammillaria, which is a group of small to medium-sized cacti. The genus Neomammillaria is now considered to be a synonym for Mammillaria, and therefore, Neomammillaria sanluisensis is classified under the genus Mammillaria. Mammillaria is further divided into several subgenera, with Mammillaria is a subgenus of the larger genus Mammillaria. The exact classification of Neomammillaria sanluisensis may vary depending on the source and the current classification systems.

The plant's tubers are said to have anti-inflammatory properties and are used to treat various ailments, including fever and rheumatism. intro: Neomammillaria sanluisensis, also known as the San Luis pincushion cactus, is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
